question_text: Two planets have the same mass, but Planet X has twice the radius of Planet Y. How will the gravitational field strength at the surface of Planet X compare to Planet Y?

options.label: A
options.text: Planet X will have 4 times stronger gravitational field strength

options.label: B
options.text: Planet X will have the same gravitational field strength

options.label: C
options.text: Planet X will have 1/4 the gravitational field strength

options.label: D
options.text: Planet X will have 1/2 the gravitational field strength

explanation: Gravitational field strength depends on distance from the planet's center. If Planet X has twice the radius, objects at its surface are twice as far from the center. Following the inverse square law, doubling the distance means the field strength becomes 1/(2²) = 1/4 as strong.
